prompt: I need a 'shipments' microservice for my logistics platform. The Shipment entity has: orderId (string), recipientName (string), address (string), trackingCode (string), status (pending|in_transit|delivered|failed). Use cases: create shipment, get shipment by id, mark as in_transit, mark as delivered, mark as failed. For infrastructure I'm using: DynamoDB as the only database (no Postgres, no MongoDB, no CQRS split), SQS for messaging (not Kafka), gRPC as the inbound protocol (not HTTP/Express/NestJS), and a simple in-memory Map as cache (no Redis). Use OpenTelemetry for tracing. Generate the full service with hexagonal architecture.

assertions.name: ports-are-abstract-classes
assertions.description: All ports use abstract class, not interface

assertions.name: use-cases-wrap-telemetry-span
assertions.description: Every use case wraps execute() in telemetry.span()

assertions.name: dynamodb-adapter-not-prisma
assertions.description: Database adapter uses DynamoDB, not Prisma or MongoDB

assertions.name: sqs-adapter-not-kafka
assertions.description: Messaging adapter uses SQS, not Kafka

assertions.name: grpc-inbound-not-express
assertions.description: Inbound adapter uses gRPC, not Express or NestJS

assertions.name: in-memory-cache-not-redis
assertions.description: Cache adapter uses in-memory Map, not Redis

assertions.name: status-as-const
assertions.description: Status enum defined as const object

assertions.name: transition-methods-on-entity
assertions.description: Entity has transition methods (markInTransit, markDelivered, markFailed or equivalent)
